about.anomaly: this is an anomaly, click at your own risk!
about.body: This OS is the free and open-source portfolio of Xavier Champoux, a technical artist based in Montreal. It was built around the idea of representing who he is, including his different expertises and passions, through the visual identity and content of the website. The code is available on GitHub, so feel free to check it out! 👀
about.title: about the OS

chat.ai1: Great Idea! I can help you create a theme for this OS using cards. Knowing he likes card games, I recommend using this card template...
chat.ai2: Got it! Here's a showcase of some of the projects done, filled in the card template and styled to look good. I hope you like it! 😄
chat.ai3: Of course! I've added some cool hovering effects on the cards to make them more interactive and visually appealing. Give it a try! 👉
chat.human1: Create a theme for this OS using cards.
chat.human2: Fill the cards with my projects.
chat.human3: Could you also add some cool hovering effects on the cards?

context.description: As he always tells people, he is not an expert in any field, but his expertise, knowledge, and curiosity make him an important asset within a team of artists and developers. Through his versatility, fluent communication and workflows emerge between all the experts.
context.subtitle: a technical artist?
context.title: Multiple cards in hands

decks.hint: decks are used to group projects
decks.title: available decks

intro.ai: and me! its integrated A.I. assistant 👋
intro.subtitle: an os about

frontenddev.description: As much as I respect React, I fell in love with how intuitive it is to work with Vue. This led me to Nuxt and the incredible suite of tools and community powering this framework (looking at you VoidZero and Unjs) which I constantly try explore and experiment with.
frontenddev.label: Frontend Development

gamedev.description: This one's obvious. Gamer since my childhood, this was the best way to combine my passions for games, arts, and programming. I have touched a few game engines along the way, but Unreal Engine really caught my attention and still continues to amaze me.
gamedev.label: Game Development

generalist3d.description: It started as some experiments in Maya and other various 3D software until I ended up falling in love with Blender, especially since the arrival of geometry nodes. My main interests are simulation and sculpting but I also enjoy working on the other aspects of 3D
generalist3d.label: 3D Generalist

programmer.description: Behind almost every one of my skills is programming. I've come accross multiple programming languages overtime, but the Typescript ecosystem is definitely my go to, even for non web related projects. I won't lie though, Rust is kind of winking at me.
programmer.label: Programming

uiuxdesigner.description: I have always been passionate about the aesthetics of websites and applications, which naturally led me to UI/UX design. Combined with my frontend development skills, this is the perfect combo to create programming aware designs that are easy to implement.
uiuxdesigner.label: UI/UX Designer

messages.1: Loading a blind monk and other champions
messages.2: Loading a galaxy with only one purple saber
messages.11: Loading mon québec la place que j'ai choisi
messages.12: Loading an orange mushroom (and pink bean...)
messages.15: Loading le coeur à ses raisons
messages.16: Loading the escape to the room

footer.design: design and <> by him, not me

motives.body: Long time fan of Star Wars and futuristic dystopian settings like Blade Runner and Cyberpunk 2077, I tend to criticize through my works the constant evolution of humans and its technology, how it is becoming an extension of our mind and body.{br}{br}Seeing the current state of our society I truly believe it is the best time to reflect on ourselves and our usage of said tools (looking at you A.I.). As a creator of dreams, I can only imagine a world where we live in harmony and peace, but at what cost?
motives.subtitle: motives
motives.title: fiction at its peak

origins.body: It all started in 2009 when I wanted to become a big blue human after crying of fear before even entering the cinema. At that moment I knew what I wanted to do: Create dreams.{br}{br}Originated from Saint-Hyacinthe, I studied visual arts before moving closer to Montreal in order to pursue my passion in VFX.
origins.subtitle: origins
origins.title: a dreamer was born

tragicEnding.ai: to the {best} (or the {worst}) my human friend

turningPoint.body: During university, my interest slowly turned towards web and game development when I came across the beauty of programming.{br}{br}Already in love with the concepts of simulation, procedural generation and algorithms, I now had the last key to the puzzle for creating the ultimate tool set. (UNLIMITED POWAAHHH!!)
turningPoint.subtitle: turning point
turningPoint.title: from artist to dev

header.title: My projects
